enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking whether courage exists or is just self-interest and foolishness. He defines courage as following the 'higher law' — what is universally right — even at great personal cost, and distinguishes it sharply from bravery, which he sees as fearless action that may or may not serve the greater good. He illustrates the concept through Roman and Byzantine history, modern marijuana legalization, Bob Lazar, and political corruption.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Courage definitely exists and should not be dismissed as self-interest or foolishness
Courage is following the higher moral law even when everyone around you is hypnotized into following the false law
Bravery is fearless action with social reward; courage is doing what's right with no reward, recognition, or support
Rome fell because people lacked courage to follow the law and uphold democratic institutions over personal safety
Weed should be legal; openly being a stoner is a small act of courage against an unjust law
Politicians are controlled by donors, not voters; policy does not reflect what the majority actually wants
Pessimism is corrosive and self-fulfilling; pessimistic people accomplish nothing and discourage others
Society should require every citizen to publicly express political opinions to normalize dissent and reduce mob suppression
